entry.title: Demystifying SAP Service Cloud v2 REST APIs: Everything You Need to Know
entry.content:
Hi,

Do you want to unlock the full potential of SAP Service Cloud Version 2 (v2) APIs? Look no further than its robust REST APIs! These APIs provide a powerful way to integrate SAP Service Cloud v2 with SAP Build Apps, external applications, SAP Build Process Automation (automate workflows), and extend functionalities to perfectly suit your business needs.

In this blog post, we'll dive deep into the world of SAP Service Cloud v2 REST APIs. We'll cover:

  • What are REST APIs and how do they benefit SAP Service Cloud v2 users?
  • Exploring the available REST APIs for core functionalities like managing cases, tickets, and customer interactions.
  • How to get started with using REST APIs for data access and manipulation.
  • Best practices and considerations for securing and managing your API integrations.
  • Real-world examples of how businesses are leveraging REST APIs to enhance their service experience.

By the end of this post, you'll be equipped with the knowledge and resources to:

  • Understand the potential of SAP Service Cloud v2 REST APIs.
  • Identify use cases for integrating SAP Service Cloud v2 with your existing ecosystem.
  • Begin exploring and utilizing the APIs for automation and customization.

SAP Sales Cloud and Service Cloud Version 2 API Documentation

I will list out all the API endpoints for your reference to automate or to integrate the data to external applications.
 
API for Registered Product Service 
Manage entities from the registered product service
API for Registered Product Categories
Manage entities from the registered product categories

API for Functional Location Service

Manage entities from the functional location service
API for Warranty Service
Manage entities from the warranty service
API for Case Service
Manage objects from case service.
API for Appointment Service
Manage entities from the appointment service.
API for Task Service
Manage entities from the task service
 
 

Stay tuned for the next part of this blog series, where I will provide a step-by-step guide on getting started with SAP Service Cloud v2 REST APIs with SAP Build Apps!

In the meantime, feel free to share your thoughts and questions in the comments below. Happy integrating!

entry.title: Re: How can we find which tables contain the field ZZEBELN.
entry.content: Excellent. NB: SE15 = repository info system = SE84 (I didn't know SE15 used to exist)

entry.title: Re: How can I add custom fields (CI_PRPS fields) to CNMASS?
entry.content:
Not in "Discussions".

Only in "Q&A".

entry.title: Re: How can we find which tables contain the field ZZEBELN.
entry.content:
To complete the good answer by @TMRajapaksha here are SE15 / SE84 screenshots (NB: today, Community don't let me paste screenshots in comments, it's why I post an answer):

After clicking the "+" button to display more criteria:

entry.title: Re: How to generate a subroutine pool(MMCP****) in SAP ?
entry.content: Hmm it's what I said: "The generated program has name MMCP5XXX or MMCP6XXX where XXX are the characters 2 to 4 of the corresponding info structure (e.g. S500 -> XXX = 500)."

entry.title: Re: I am creating an IDOC via transaction F110 for the ve...
entry.content: Please don't repeat your question, just edit your question (add an EDIT chapter because many people have already read your question). Also, in general, don't post a question as a solution.

entry.title: Re: I am creating an IDOC via transaction F110 for the vendor , message type PAYEXT. How to debug it
entry.content: Debug may be tricky for unexperimented people. Just to be sure if one exit is called or not, you may use ABAP code at the start of the exit (e.g. MESSAGE x001(00)) to generate a short dump in ST22. You are then sure whether the exit is called or not.

entry.title: Re: How to Access Questions Related to a specific tag on SAP community
entry.content: Already asked here -> https://community.sap.com/t5/questions-about-sap-websites/how-do-i-search-for-questions-and-blog-posts-that-have-a-particular-tag/qaq-p/833609

entry.title: Re: SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP Developer Edition Installation Pitfalls + Video Tutorial
entry.content: @Ahmed101 You should ask the question here: Technology Q&A | SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM Landscape configuration
entry.content:
Thank you very much!  The link you sent is extremely helpful, and answered my questions.

Also, if you happen to have any information on how to set up a 3 system landscape for Cloud ALM in a Private Cloud Environment then do please share.

entry.title: Re: How to send data from Internal table to File in App. Server
entry.content:
Check out this report

 

REPORT ZK_FILE_TO_APPL_SERVER.

TABLES : KNA1.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*     Data Decalaration
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
DATA: GT_KNA1 TYPE TABLE OF KNA1,
      GS_KNA1 TYPE KNA1.
DATA: GV_FILE   TYPE RLGRAP-FILENAME.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK B1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-001.
SELECT-OPTIONS : S_KUNNR FOR KNA1-KUNNR OBLIGATORY.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK B1.


*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*     START-OF-SELECTION
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
PERFORM GET_DATA.

IF NOT GT_KNA1[] IS INITIAL.
  PERFORM SAVE_FILE.
ELSE.
  MESSAGE 'No data found' TYPE 'I'.
ENDIF.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  get_data
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM GET_DATA.
*Get data
  SELECT * FROM KNA1
         INTO TABLE GT_KNA1 WHERE KUNNR IN S_KUNNR.
ENDFORM.                    " get_data
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  save_file
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM SAVE_FILE.
  DATA: LV_DATA TYPE STRING.

*Move complete path to filename
*  gv_file = '/tmp/cust.txt'.
  "Note if we are not passing any specific directory, by default it will
  " get stored in DIR_HOME in AL11 transaction

  CONCATENATE '/tmp/' SY-UNAME SY-DATUM SY-UZEIT '.txt' INTO GV_FILE.


* Open the file in output mode
  OPEN DATASET GV_FILE FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT.
  IF SY-SUBRC NE 0.
    MESSAGE 'Unable to create file' TYPE 'I'.
    EXIT.
  ENDIF.

  LOOP AT GT_KNA1 INTO GS_KNA1.
    CONCATENATE GS_KNA1-KUNNR
                GS_KNA1-ANRED
                GS_KNA1-NAME1
                GS_KNA1-LAND1
                GS_KNA1-REGIO
                GS_KNA1-CITYC
     INTO LV_DATA
     SEPARATED BY ','.
*TRANSFER moves the above fields from workarea to file  with comma
*delimited format
    TRANSFER LV_DATA TO GV_FILE.
    CLEAR: GS_KNA1.
  ENDLOOP.
* close the file
  CLOSE DATASET GV_FILE.

  CONCATENATE 'File' GV_FILE 'created' INTO DATA(GV_MSG) SEPARATED BY SPACE.

  MESSAGE GV_MSG TYPE 'I'.

ENDFORM.                    " save_file

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear Mr. @David Chaviano,

My name is Minh Tuan, and I am currently a third-year student in Vietnam. My future career aspiration is to become a SAP Consultant. After spending time researching and studying various learning journeys on the learning.sap website, I have found the content to be incredibly engaging and practical. Therefore, I am eagerly hoping to receive a voucher for the SAP certificate exam. I firmly believe that obtaining the SAP certificate wil validate my accumulated knowledge and greatly assist me in my future career. I pledge to persevere in my studies and approach the exam with utmost seriousness.

Thank you, and I wish you a wonderful day.

Best regards,
Tuan

entry.title: KSA eInvoice - DRC - Downpayment for Direct FI Invoice cases from SAP DRC for KSA
entry.content:
How to handle scenarios of downpayment in case of Direct FI Invoice as no link is between DP and Invoice. Clearing has to be performed first to ensure DP is considered from overall Invoice value.

However, eInvoice is created in Real time from SAP DRC, hence it will result in dual reporting to ZATCA.

 

Also does DRC provides useable WHT return for KSA. Need clarity on maturity of the report.

entry.title: Re: What's your favorite tip for using SAP Build Apps?
entry.content:
That's great 👏

More generally, you can always drag a flow function on the canvas and in the upper right click "Read More".

Then it will tell you what each of the outputs is for.

entry.title: No process found for the Consignment with the code: a0000xxx_ordermanagement
entry.content:
hi guys,

when i login backoffice with  a warehousing agent role to check consignment created along with order, shows error msg in backend 

No process found for the Consignment with the code

i want to know why defaultwarehousingshippingservice needs to check consignment process code with "xxx_ordermanagement", there is no such process code, where and when will create one?

please help me with this issue.

thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Static images are not displayed correctly in SAP Android preview
entry.content:

  • Are these images uploaded to Asset Manager and then used in Containers and Image components?
  • Are these images uploaded by the user?
  • Are these icons related to the Android app?
  • Or something else?

Screenshots always help 😸

Are you saying you immediately (within 10 seconds) open the preview and type in the code and it constantly gives you a session timeout message?

Can somebody help over here

entry.title: SAP Project System - GL Account is Invalid
entry.content:
Hello everyone,


In SAP Project System, in transaction CJ20N, when I assign a material component to an internal activity and choose my material, then I select "L" stock item for this material and I choose the following procurement type to generate a PR: PReq + Reservation for WBS Element. Afterwards, I enter the requirement quantity for this material component and then I hit enter on the keyboard to finalize the creation of this material component. I realize that the GL account assigned to this material automatically changes, from 3090300 (GBB-->VBR) to 1211000 (BSX) and an error is shown which says "GL account 1211000 is invalid". I can't save the progress for my project because when I do there is error in calculating costs since the system saw an error in this material component. What could be the cause of that?

Note: My project stock is maintained as valuated stock in the project profile configuration.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: RAP service UI is not working but OData returning response
entry.content: In the OData preview screenshot there is a TravelID=1 filter. What happens when you remove the filter? In the table settings (gear icon) ensure that at least one column is selected.

entry.title: Re: RAP service UI is not working but OData returning response
entry.content: In the OData preview screenshot there is a TravelID=1 filter. What happens when you remove the filter? In the table settings (gear icon) ensure that at least one column is selected.

entry.title: Re: RAP: Creating a child entity with 'create by association' for an existing root entity fails
entry.content: I would recommend to define the action as factory action.

entry.title: Re: Managing Security Roles across S/4HANA Public Cloud a...
entry.content:
Hello,

Its not clear if you are already using GRC? If so and you still like to leverage GRC for these S/4HANA cloud and success factor systems you can adopt IAG Bridge solution, there are some nice blogs in the community but below I find to be helpful

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/iag-bridge-scenario-grc-ac-12-0-integration-with-iag/ba-p/13564421

If still not using GRC, you may think of the IAG as a stand alone solution for your cloud systems. Below is standard documentation from SAP

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CLOUD_IDENTITY_ACCESS_GOVERNANCE

Regards,

Prana

entry.title: Re: Page variable binded to boolean doesn't work
entry.content:
I was able to do exactly what you wanted with the page variable. I even created the third-party authentication, which I assume you used, and created it with those components/page. Not sure what is happening for you. 

When you say nothing happens, do you see the spinner and the login screen at the beginning? Can you show with Alert the value of the boolean (you'll have to use a formula and IF function)?

ie values are

I see the learning journey of this still existsProvision and Administer Databases in SAP HANA Cloud | SAP Learning

and this learning journey say leads to certification. SAP Certification

 

So which certification does this leads to? because I don't want to spend hours of learning time which leads to nothing.
So, if anyone know the information on this please comment.

Thanks, in advance.
Regards,
Revanth Kanteerava

entry.title: Material master labels print out transcation
entry.content:
Hello Everyone 

Is there any way to mass print material master labels outside the PPF and goods movement process , The issue I have is when material is received in multiple quantities only 1 label will print but each material must be labeled with the MM label , users will have to go through the PPF , GI, GR process to print more labels or create labels maually which is in both cases time consumming . 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports Group heading text change
entry.content:
You seem to be using an assignment operator (:=) instead of equal check (=).

For example, 

if {_PM_OSCT.U_Branch} := '53'

should be changed to :

if {_PM_OSCT.U_Branch} = '53'

entry.content:
Hi @Sanil Bhandari,

We are on ECC6,

I am looking to change this under AU (Australia)

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Minimum Pay/Wage concept in SuccessFactors Time Tracking
entry.content:
Background:

Some organizations that I have come across have a minimum wage setup. For instance, if an employee has a 8 hour work schedule Mon – Fri, they get a minimum pay equivalent to working 4 hours even if they spend 5 minutes working for that day. And further beyond 4 hours, they get the regular pay for the number of hours they work in that day.

 

How can we set this up in Time Valuation?

You might already have some of your own ways to achieve this. I found a new way of dealing with this with a new time valuation method which was introduced in 2311 i.e using Create Time Record Method. Hence, I thought of sharing the same if it helps you in any way.

Let me illustrate how this can be done in a simpler way.

 

The below is a time recording profile (Clock Times) that I am going to use to achieve this.

The first three evaluations are very self explanatory and most of this you would already know. They get recorded working time and deduct any unpaid breaks from them. Hence I would not get into details of them. Essentially the starting point would be recorded times without breaks.

 

Now the main business starts from 4th evaluation until the last one

Create a dummy 4 hour record as a sample

It can have any start time and end time as fixed value as long the duration is 4 hour

In the above example, I have it from 6:00 AM to 10:00 AM

Check Min wage/pay 4 Hours

In this time valuation we will check if recorded times excluding breaks is below the 4 hour threshold or not. It uses a compare threshold with comparison group and route input type.

If net recorded working times is less than 4 , we route the input (which is also net recorded times) to Time type group below where we store hours recorded below 4 hours for further minimum wage/pay check. If recorded hours are more than 4, then we move them to Time type group above where we pay the regular wage for the employee as per the hours worked for that day. The TTG Above CLT-PAY-REG is set as time pay type for payroll

 

Pay Min 4 Hours If Required

In this final time evaluation, we actually pay the minimum pay of 4 hours if the employee has worked less than 4 hours for that day. This too usesa compare threshold with comparison group and route input type.

As you can see , we compare the results of previous time valuation (where we stored the hours below 4 hours threshold in TTG) as a comparison group and we compare that with the threshold 0 hours.

If CLT- Store Below 4 hours is > 0, it indicates that employee has minimum 1 minute of recorded time for that day. In that case we route the input time type group Min 4 HR Record or dummy 4 HR record to Time type group above CLT – Final pay min wage where employee is paid a minimum wage or pay equivalent of 4 hours for that day.

 

Now let’s test this out

Testing

 

The employee works for just 1 hour for a day

But still gets 4 hours paid out. The minimum wage/pay concept has triggered here

Next, the employee works for 6 hours in a day. He/She gets standard pay for 6 hours as seen below

This is one of the simpler ways of achieving this requirement which may sound a bit complex to begin with.

Hope the new dimension of “CREATE TIME RECORD” time valuation is unearthed here and you use it in your time valuations

Would be happy to hear about your feedback

Thanks

Neelesh

entry.title: COPA reversal documents
entry.content:
Hi,

We have reversed COPA documents manually by using KE4S00. But while doing it we missed some documents to reverse and we reversed some documents that are already reversed.

1.how to know what are the documents that are reversed initially and 

2. how to know what are the documents that are reversed twice.

Is there any way to find out ? Can we find the reversed document in any table ?

Please suggest.  Thanks @SAPSupport

entry.content:

=> Copy program structure.
=> carry forward mesaure/app req.
=>carry forwards budget values:
I need reverse the FY that I have opened. 
I have tried to deleted it in IM22 but it is giving error saying budget is assigned already. Please advise.
We are on ECC,

Thanks in advance
Stalin

entry.title: Re: CPI Post Method with Odata V2 Adapter
entry.content: <ET_EMPSet xml:base="http://Win12SAPHANA.dccil.com:8001/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZEMP_DATA_UPLD_SRV/" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom" xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata" xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices"> <category term="ZEMP_DATA_UPLD_SRV.ET_EMP" scheme="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/scheme"/> <content type="application/xml"> <m:properties> <EmpId>${header.EmpId}</EmpId> <EmpName>${header.EmpName}</EmpName> <EmpSal>${header.EmpSal}</EmpSal> </m:properties> </content> </ET_EMPSet>

entry.title: Hana Backup deletion option for native backups solution
entry.content:
are taking backups on local disk for some time , here backups are loading to that location and not deleting any of the backups. Is there any option to delete those backup files ? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Hana Backup deletion option for native backups solution
entry.content:
To clean up older backups and remove them, you can do so via HANA studio
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/fa0f4cccdb354ed5b45d661ed52692d3.html?version=2.0.05

or via HANA cockpit
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/7c8ceb2924414f58b522aacebab55001.html?version=2.0.05

You can also create a retention policy to automatically delete old backups :
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_COCKPIT/afa922439b204e9caf22c78b6b69e4f2/b495da9a043a499893abc50c5cd367d9.html

Add for EC integration with S4HANA is PA SE IN

from S4HANA 2022 once H4S4 is switched on, we recommend to use latest version ECS4HCM

Refer below

 

Upgrading from PA_SE_IN to ECS4HCM for Data Replication from Employee Central

entry.title: Farewell to an Era: The End of an Iconic Certification of SAP BODS - C_DS_42
entry.content:
Hello All,

 

continuing to the previous blogs on SAP DS 4.2 and 4.3

  1. End of ERA - SAP BODS 4.2
  2. SAP BODS / Data services road map DS 4.3 | SAP Blogs
  3. SAP Data services (BODS) 4.3 release / Installation / new features of BODS 4.3 | SAP Blogs

Today, we're diving into a topic near and dear to the hearts of many in the data services realm: the legendary certification, C_DS_42, for BODS 4.2. For over a decade, this certification has been the holy grail for data services consultants, a badge of honor symbolizing expertise and proficiency in SAP's Data Services platform.

However, as with all good things, its time has come to an end. On the 28th of March 2024, the C_DS_42 certification officially expired, marking the end of an era for many professionals who have cherished and pursued it.

What makes this transition even more surprising is the fact that its successor, C_DS_43, the certification for the latest version of Data Services, has also met the same fate, entering the realm of expired certifications.

For those who have dedicated countless hours to mastering BODS 4.2 and preparing for the C_DS_42 exam, this news may come as a bittersweet moment. It's a reminder of the ever-evolving nature of technology and the necessity of staying current in a field where innovation waits for no one.

But fear not, for every ending marks a new beginning. While bidding farewell to C_DS_42 may evoke a sense of nostalgia, it also presents an opportunity for renewal and growth. As the industry moves forward, so must we.

So what's next? For those who held C_DS_42 with pride, it's time to embark on a new journey. Whether it's upgrading to the latest version of Data Services or exploring other avenues within the data management landscape, there are plenty of opportunities to expand our horizons and continue making a meaningful impact.

As we reflect on the legacy of C_DS_42, let's remember the skills and knowledge it has imparted upon us. Let's honor the hard work and dedication that went into achieving this certification, and let's carry that same passion into the next chapter of our professional lives.

Farewell, C_DS_42. You may be expired, but your legacy lives on in the countless professionals you've inspired along the way.

Here's to new beginnings and the endless possibilities that lie ahead.

Thanks,

Venkatesh Golla

entry.title: IAS User Sync
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

What are the advantages and recommended use cases for synchronizing users from Azure AD to SAP IAS ?

 

Many thanks

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Farewell to an Era: The End of an Iconic Certification of SAP BODS - C_DS_42
entry.content:
1. End of ERA - SAP BODS 4.2
  2. SAP BODS / Data services road map DS 4.3 | SAP Blogs
  3. SAP Data services (BODS) 4.3 release / Installation / new features of BODS 4.3 | SAP Blogs

entry.title: Re: Provision users from Microsoft Azure AD to SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication
entry.content:
Hello Harjeet,

is it advisable or practical to synchronize users from Azure Active Directory (AD) to SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) in a simple installation setup, such as:

Azure - IAS - SAP BAS or SAP SAC.

Before making this decision, I'm eager to hear recommendations from SAP or experts in the field.

Are there any best practices or specific scenarios where synchronizing users between Azure AD and SAP IAS is particularly beneficial?

Conversely, are there any reasons why this might not be advisable?

Many thanks


Best regards

entry.title: Re: sap abap
entry.content:
You probably installed ABAP trial which contains only the basis components of ABAP-based software.

MARA and EKPO tables are part of SAP ERP or S/4HANA, which is made of the basis components + the ERP components. You are probably interested by such software with demonstration data, so you should install either IDES (you can find the different versions available to you in the SAP Support portal) or via appliance (2041140 - Order an SAP S/4HANA fully-activated appliance for on-premise deployment - SAP for Me "Request the delivery of the appliance by creating a support ticket on the component XX-SER-SWFL-SHIP...")

Also see this answer in 2022: Availability SAP S/4HANA IDES - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: SAP Certified Application Associate - SAP Signavio - Exam Retiring
entry.content: Jocelyn! Ifound 2 new certification that replace the one that was retired! Check them!

entry.title: Re: Certify Your Skills as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Consultant
entry.content:
Dear Dieter,

I read your blog post and all the comments.

I am appearing for C_S4CPB_2402 certification (Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) in 2 weeks. I have completed my course learning "Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Consultants". As mentioned in your comments, I have now started course "Exploring SAP Cloud ERP". 

I am still not confident about the exams and answering the questions adequately. Anything else that you suggest covering - any mock exams, any other course etc.

Any guidance would be helpful. 

Thanks

Amit

entry.title: Re: Stock Type Determination of Inbound Delivery in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM)
entry.content: Thank you @saurabh_nigade - Your inputs to this blog are an icing on the cake.

entry.title: Re: sy-subrc = 4 for select statement but SE16N have records
entry.content: Use leading zeros in hkont.

entry.title: SAP Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, Private Cloud is here!
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the release of Feature Package 01 for SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management 2023. This release demonstrates our continued commitment to empowering businesses to improve their supply chain execution and run their transportation operations efficiently.

Key features in this release include:

  1. Integration innovations:
    1. Sales order scheduling – a much anticipated innovation that allows a customer service representative to use data from Transportation Management when determining the delivery date for the sales order. For more details see this blog.
    2. MM Scheduling Agreement – this innovation allows a more selective pull of schedule lines into TM resulting in performance improvements since only relevant schedule lines are integrated. For more details see this blog.
    3. Incoterms support - for certain incoterms (e.g., EXW and FCA) changes were made to ensures ASNs are sent when the transfer of risk occurs.
    4. Advanced handling when warehouse loading stops are not completed. This process helps planners manage the exception process including the unassignment of cargo that was not loaded and the re-assignment of the cargo for later pickup.
    5. Event based customer invoice - it is now possible to automatically trigger the customer invoice when a defined event has posted reducing manual efforts.
    6. The left behind at the port process simplifies the process of re-assigning containers to another booking when they are not loaded on the ship as planned, simplifying the exception process for transportation planners.
  2. Planning & optimization:
    1. Routing can now be configured to include or exclude routes with tolls and routing quality is improved with the addition of a report to identify any triangle inequalities.
    2. Drive time and working constraints such as rests are considered by planning and scheduling and are visible in the Gantt chart. As a result, more accurate and executable trips that follow relevant driver safety regulations can be created.
    3. When creating a package unit, the packaging material can be selected at the same time, simplifying the process and improving usability for transportation planners.
  3. Collaboration innovations:
    1. The procurement process was improved so carriers can submit multiple bids in a single auction while also seeing the current lowest rates.  On the shipper side, for those managing RFQ’s it is now easier to create and monitor RFQs: multiple carriers can now be mass-inserted in a single step and RFQ’s are automatically updated upon receipt of confirmations.
    2. Carriers can now submit deviating time window information (revised ETA’s) via the collaboration apps which will update the freight order details which are then available to other parts of the organization (e.g., customer service).
  4. Other innovations:
    1. Improvements to the freight order interface enable sending and receiving freight orders with attachments, allowing shippers and carriers to share documents relevant to a specific freight order. 
    2. In the settlement area, there is now the ability to configure the settlement process that allows changes to freight orders after the accruals process and invoices are posted.  This simplifies how changes are shared between TM and financial accounting.

For further details on the innovations included in SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management 2023 FPS1 refer to the What’s New Blog.

This feature pack (FPS1) is the first release in the new cadence of feature packs being released every six-months between the major releases. The next scheduled feature pack for the 2023 release is scheduled for Q4 of 2024.

At SAP, we are dedicated to driving innovations and delivering solutions that empower businesses to thrive in today’s dynamic supply chain environment. To learn more about SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Transportation Management private edition, please contact your SAP Account Executive.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:

  • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
  • First generation college students
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

Maciej

entry.title: Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:

I recently came across the post about the Digital Skills Initiative and I'm writing to express my interest in developing my skills on this journey and at the end have an attempt at a free certificate.

I am a black woman, and a person with a disability, and at 44 years old transitioning to an IT career. I will be exploring the learning journeys in SAP Learning, and I want to delve deeper into the module, and soon the certificate would be a great opportunity for me to evolve and prove my efforts.

Waiting for your answer.

Yours sincerely!

entry.title: Re: First steps using the Hana Vector Engine with SAP GEN AI
entry.content:
Hey!!! amazing blog!!! I can create the DB; the embeddings but when execute the embeed step I have this error. 

 

alueError                                Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[13], line 25
     23     content = p
     24     model_dim = 'ADA_1536'   ## OPENAI ADA ... Dimensions 1536
---> 25     embedding =  get_sap_genai_ada_embedding(content)
     27     csv_rows.append([i,content,model_dim,embedding])
     29 ### insert csv rows into Embedding Content

Cell In[10], line 2, in get_sap_genai_ada_embedding(input)
      1 def get_sap_genai_ada_embedding(input) -> str:
----> 2   response = embeddings.create(
      3       input=input,
      4       model_name="text-embedding-ada-002",
      5       #encoding_format='base64'
      6   )
      7   return str(response.data[0].embedding)

File ~/anaconda3/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gen_ai_hub/proxy/native/openai/clients.py:88, in Embeddings.create(self, input, model, deployment_id, model_name, config_id, config_name, **kwargs)
     81 model_name = if_set(model_name, if_set(model))
     82 model_identification = kwargs_if_set(
     83     deployment_id=deployment_id,
     84     model_name=model_name,
     85     config_id=config_id,
     86     config_name=config_name,
     87 )
---> 88 deployment = proxy_client.select_deployment(**model_identification)
     89 model_name = deployment.model_name or '???'
     90 with set_deployment(deployment):

File ~/anaconda3/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gen_ai_hub/proxy/core/utils.py:68, in lru_cache_extended.<locals>.decorator.<locals>.wrapped_func(_recache, *args, **kwargs)
     66     objs[id(obj)] = obj
     67 try:
---> 68     ret = cached_method(cache_key, *args, **kwargs)
     69 finally:
     70     if first_arg_self:

File ~/anaconda3/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gen_ai_hub/proxy/core/utils.py:58, in lru_cache_extended.<locals>.decorator.<locals>.cached_method(cache_key, *args, **kwargs)
     55     id_, _ = cache_key
     56     args = (objs.pop(id_),) + (args or tuple([]))
---> 58 return func(*args, **kwargs)

File ~/anaconda3/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gen_ai_hub/proxy/gen_ai_hub_proxy/client.py:182, in GenAIHubProxyClient.select_deployment(self, raise_on_multiple, **search_key_value)
    180 except IndexError:
    181     pass
--> 182 raise ValueError('No deployment found with: '
    183                  + ', '.join([f'deployment.{k} == {v}' for k, v in search_key_value.items()]))

ValueError: No deployment found with: deployment.model_name == text-embedding-ada-002


Do you have any idea about this?? I

entry.title: Solman 7.2 on HANA 2.0 SP7 Rev 072 RHEL 9.0 to RHEL 9.2
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a SAP Solman 7.2 system (Application - Kernel 754_REL Patch 200 and HANA DB 2.0 SP7 Rev 072) installed on RHEL 9.0. Our OS team is planning to upgrade RHEL to 9.2. Please advise if Solman 7.2 system (Application - Kernel 754_REL Patch 200 and HANA DB 2.0 SP7 Rev 072) is supported on RHEL 9.2.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Solman 7.2 on HANA 2.0 SP7 Rev 072 RHEL 9.0 to RHEL 9.2
entry.content:
Hello,

 

Could you please check SAP note 2369910 - SAP Software on Linux: General information, which contains the following statement:

---------------

Update of RPM packages/implementation of security updates

"The distributors (SUSE, Red Hat, and Oracle) provide regular updates for their software, for example due to security reasons. The distributors test these updates with regard to compatibility with the SAP software before releasing them. From an SAP point of view, these updates can be imported in the productive environment immediately after they are available. This compatibility is guaranteed only if the original packages of the distributors are used, to which a customer is entitled within the framework of a valid support contract with the distributor or an authorized OEM.

The same also applies for the "Collections of updates", that is "Service Packs" (SUSE), "Minor Releases" (Red Hat), and "Updates" (Oracle Linux).

We recommend that you run the latest available "Service Pack" (SUSE), the latest "Minor Release" (Red Hat), or the latest "Update" (Oracle Linux) with the current online updates. You should use the latest available installation media when setting up an new system.

If an update does not work expectedly, return to the last "Service Pack", "Minor Release", or "Update" that worked."

-------------

 

So, given the above, if your SolMan 7.2 is certified on RedHat 9.0, it is automatically certified on all subsequent RedHat 9 updates, including 9.2.

 

Best regards,

Leo

SAP Support

We are not able to add attachments to the Job Definition. Please find the steps to reproduce the issue:

  1. Go to Organizational Management > Job Definition.
  2. Select one of the job definitions.
  3. Hit on Edit button.
  4. Hit on Add button, and add a file.
  5. It's showing "No Data" and we are not able to upload the attachment.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Not able to add attachments to Job Definition

We are working on a fix for this, and the fix is expected to be deployed in 2405 release.

Please also refer to Release Schedule and Dates for 2024

Hi, Expert,

Would you please explain what's the function for "mat.grouping" in view "Work Scheduling"

RGDS

Joanna Qiu

entry.title: Z, gz, ZIP: A bit of a compression eyechart
entry.content:
As I browse online analyses of a compromised compression algorithm library, my mind drifted back to the early days of free UNIX and open source software, particularly the "birth" of GNU Z, the LZW algorithm, and the refreshing alternative "zip."

My first UNIX experience was a Unisys AT&T System VR2, then later VR3, which among its other built-in software had the compression program aptly named "compress" that tool typically text files and shrank them to a tenth of the usual storage space. You may still (or "again" depending on your timeline) find this inside a range of operating systems. Like this:

 

[...]
EXIT STATUS
     The compress utility exits 0 on success, and >0 if an error occurs.

SEE ALSO
     zcat(1)
     Welch, Terry A., "A Technique for High Performance Data Compression",
     IEEE Computer, 17:6, pp. 8-19, June, 1984.
HISTORY
     The compress command appeared in 4.3BSD.

NetBSD 10.0                    January 23, 2003                    NetBSD 10.0

 

Terry Welch had the "W" in the LZW protocol and worked at Sperry (one of the former companies that became Unisys). Because of law-stuff you can research, the distribution of this handy utility became problematic, so the GNU open source developers created an alternative tool that could (a) uncompress ".Z" files, (b) ran faster, (c) compressed more, and (d) was copy-lefted. Named gzip instead of compress, it liberated us from law-stuff, mostly.

 

[...]
EXIT STATUS
     The gzip utility exits 0 on success, 1 on errors, and 2 if a warning
     occurs.
SIGNALS
     gzip responds to the following signals:
     SIGINFO
             Report progress to standard error.
SEE ALSO
     bzip2(1), compress(1), xz(1), fts(3), zlib(3)
HISTORY
     The gzip program was originally written by Jean-loup Gailly, licensed
     under the GNU Public Licence.  Matthew R. Green wrote a simple front end
     for NetBSD 1.3 distribution media, based on the freely re-distributable
     zlib library.  It was enhanced to be mostly feature-compatible with the
     original GNU gzip program for NetBSD 2.0.
     This manual documents NetBSD gzip version 20170803.
AUTHORS
     This implementation of gzip was written by Matthew R. Green

 

This man page mentions "zlib", for reference, also a handy base library for compression utilities.

 

SEE ALSO
     RFC 1950      ZLIB Compressed Data Format Specification.
     RFC 1951      DEFLATE Compressed Data Format Specification.
     RFC 1952      GZIP File Format Specification.
     zlib: http://www.gzip.org/zlib/

 

Once system administrators got wind of "gz" versus "Z", some of us went with the former, others who needed to report to stricter management could not. I remember hacking away at a Prime system that we got to supplant the Unisys UNIX host, with a bit of BSD and a bit of AT&T. Getting the GCC compiler to work was needed first, usually, as the stock K&R tools were not sufficient. Fixing compile time errors by removing or editing code got me the hairy eyeball from a peer, but I got gzip built and on the plan.

Among the people who stood out (see, e.g. a writeup on the RFC 1950: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1950) were Jean-Loup Gailly and Mark Adler, the latter tagged as "original Zip author; UnZip decompression". One of the cool facts was the overlap of space exploration with algorithm development. Mark Adler had a day job at NASA "driving Rovers" and was hence, well positioned to leverage that research with compression code to get pictures from Mars as fast as the speed of light would allow.

I'll also mention Greg Roelofs (see: https://infozip.sourceforge.net/) who helped me when I attempted to fix something in the UNIX X legacy program xpaint. You will likely see the results of that experience when he reviewed the XPaint code base and found a terrible design flaw that mangled color palettes when editing images. You may still see that warning today (as I do):

 

XPaint uses the native display format for storing image info while editing; 
the original image information is thrown away.  This means that, in general, 
color information is irretrievably lost when using any display depth less 
than 24 bits.

 

So I like to say I didn't create this bug, or squash it, I just was lucky to work with someone who spotted the design flaw when I asked about another issue.

EYE CHART:

 

 - - - -
|   Z    |
|   gz   |
|  ZIP   |
|Info-Zip|
- - - - -

entry.title: BKP – Simplify your user input with proper variant setup via configuration activities
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Customer Returns Solution (Scope Item BKP) can handle different returns scenarios in an effective way.  The system pre-defined process variants and corresponding options empower you a variable and flexible end to end process scenarios.

By default, the process variants and their options are all activated as a maximum capability to consume. In your real customer returns business, you might just have certain returns processes which require a subset of process variant options. To simplify your user input, and improve user experience, you could utilize the available configuration activity to customize your business solutions.

Logistical follow up activity - Customer Returns (BKP Scope Item) offers a list of typical logistical follow up activities for you to consume and accelerate the logistical processing – corresponding follow up document would be auto triggered once released. 

  • Configuration activity 105526 Activate and Rename Follow-Up Activities

With this configuration, you can just activate and rename the necessary follow up activities according to your real business.

By default, all follow up activities are activated, you can deactivate some follow up activities which do not fit your real business.

For example,

  • You can de-activate 0005 and 0007 if in your customer returns, you never forward the returned product to your supplier.
  • You can de-activate 0008 and 0013 if you never have product inspection or keep the product at your customer site.
  • You can de-activate 0026 and 0027 if you don’t have in-house repair business with customer returns.

With proper activation of only needed follow up activities, you would see less options in follow up activities in returns item creation, or product inspection.  This makes it easier for your end users and ensure a precise process.

 

Refund Control - Customer Returns (BKP Scope Item) offers several refund control options to specify how and when the refund is to be processed.

Configuration activity 105716 Activate and Rename Refund Control

With this configuration, you can just activate and rename the necessary refund control according to your real business.

For example,

  • According to your refund policy, if you never have an immediate refund to customer, you can de-activate the option I Immediately.
  • Meanwhile, you can customize your meaningful description based on the two different refund types.

 

Note – for above two configuration activities, you had better de-activate the values that are not needed at the project beginning phase. If later you want to de-activate a specific value, system would check any returns order has already used the specific value.

  • For 2SL system, once a specific value is used in production system, you can’t de-activate it.
  • For 3SL system, once a specific value is used in test or production system, you can’t de-activate it.

 

Configuration activity 106585 Define Default Values for Customer Returns Apps

You could use this configuration activity to accelerate the user input in apps ‘Manage Customer Returns -Version 2’ and ‘Create Customer Return Without Reference - VA01 with default values’.

The returns process variant values can be defaulted at plant level.

With above default values, when you input the material number into the returns order item, system would set the default values for your configured variants.

You could utilize this configuration activity to reflect your main returns scenario.

 

With above configuration activities, you can improve your experience with the apps by simplifying the input.

 

Related Documents

Customer Returns at help portal

Customer Returns (BKP)

entry.title: /DSCSAG function module documentation
entry.content:
Hello members,

                      Could you please let me know if there is any document that covers the mapping between the functions within SAP ECTR configuration and the function module within SAP HANA.

For example, I would like to know the function module fnc.doc.cache.update calls within SAP.

entry.title: CDS View for "Withholding Tax Code" in supplier invoice
entry.content:
I would like to use the "Withholding tax code" entered on the supplier invoice as an entry in the newly created CDS view.

I have confirmed that the Withholding tax code set in the BP master can be used as an item in the CDS view by using Standard CDS view "I_SupplierWithHoldingTax".

I would like to use the Withholfing Tax Code value entered on the supplier invoice in the CDS view, not the Withholfing Tax Code value set in the BP master.

I tried looking for this item in the Customer Data Browser application, but could not find it.

If you know of a CDS view that includes the items I am looking for, please let me know.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CDS View for "Withholding Tax Code" in supplier invoice
entry.content:
Could you please check CDS view I_SuplrInvcHeaderWhldgTaxAPI01?

This view relates to the following SAP object types:

  • SupplierInvoice

Main CDS parameters

The main CDS parameters are:

  • SUPPLIERINVOICE used to specify the supplier invoice

  • FISCALYEAR used to specify the fiscal year

  • WITHHOLDINGTAXCODE used to specify the withholding tax code

  • WITHHOLDINGTAXBASEAMOUNT used to specify the base amount of the withholding tax

  • MANUALLYENTEREDWHLDGTAXAMOUNT used to specify the manually entered amount of the withholding tax

Please refer to this online documentation for detailed information:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/ee6ff9b281d8448f96b4fe6c89f2bdc8/de0e5df53f2d4e79a74aa9144f220f0e.html?locale=en-US

Hi All

How to solve when Hana/Logs 99% consumed?  User is not able to add new Warehouse.

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: It's a wrap!
entry.content:
Yesterday, we concluded March's developer challenge in the SAP Community - All about CloudEvents and event-driven architectures. Thanks to everyone who took part in it :-). I hope you had as much fun participating in the challenge as I had preparing it.
 
In case you missed the developer challenge but you are interested in knowing what it was all about... you can get started here: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 1.
If you want to learn more about CloudEvents and event-driven integrations, I would recommend keeping an eye on the content of the Event-driven integration CodeJam - https://github.com/SAP-samples/events-driven-integrations-codejam, which is now close to being finalised :-). Also, remember that you can request/host an SAP CodeJam event in your city or company, and we will be happy to help you run it - https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/so-you-want-to-host-a-codejam-everything-you-need-to-know/ba-p/221415.
 
Now, some cool stats on community members' participation:
  • A total of 36 SAP Community members took part in the challenge.
  • Unique SAP community members participation per week:
    WeekTotal submissions
    Week 131
    Week 226
    Week 323
    Week 416
    Total96
  • Total submissions per week. Note: In week 2 a couple of participants submitted more than one solution and in weeks 3 and 4, I'm counting as valid submissions any valid message received. Many participants submitted more than one message.
    WeekTotal submissions
    Week 131
    Week 228
    Week 370
    Week 498
    Total227
  • Last but certainly not least... a special mention to all the SAP Community members who completed the challenge every single week 🎉
    @Alpesa1990
    @MatLakaemper
    @MioYasuatke
    @Nagarajan-K
    @PriyankaChak 
    @Ruthiel 
    @ajos
    @emiliocampo
    @geek
    @pamoli_banerjee
    @r00k13d3v
    @saitgunacorel
    @spirit2681
    @xavisanse

If you are interested in the event-driven integrations/architectures topic.... 📺 stay tuned as there is way more coming later this year in this space.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Platform Integration for SAP Business ByDesign webinar
entry.content:
Hello Trinidad,

I would like to watch online webinar recording videos that you shared in this blog.

But the videos are expired to watch.

So, if you still have these videos or link available, could you please share with me.

Thanks in advance.

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition with BTP?
entry.content: Can SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition use or integrate with BTP? because I have a need to access BTP such as Web IDE etc

entry.title: Open excel instance activity of SAP Build Process Automation is inactive.
entry.content:
I am using a Mac with desktop agent version 3.25.43. While creating projects in my system, I am not able to use the excel related activities. I have the Excel SDK added in dependencies as well. My excel version is 16.83. Is there something else I need to add, or is this problem related to the Excel version causing the activities to not work?

Regards,

Remya
